arts.sum: Get the latest Coventry City news sent straight to your phone by joining our Sky Blues WhatsApp group. “Blackburn Rovers can confirm that head coach John Eustace has requested permission to speak with another Championship club, following the activation of a clause in his contract,” said Rovers in a statement.

arts.ttl: Predicted Coventry City team - Frank Lampard's key change to exert control over QPR - Andy Turner - CoventryLive
arts.sum: Predicted City team v QPR: Oliver Dovin; Joel Latibeaudiere, Bobby Thomas, Liam Kitching; Milan van Ewijk, Victor Torp, Matt Grimes, Jack Rudoni, Jay Dasilva; Brandon Thomas-Asante, Ellis Simms. Expect Milan van Ewijk to return at right wing-back in place of Joel Latibeaudiere after being rested due to fatigue against the Tractor Boys, while an adductor injury to Jake Bidwell means Jay Dasilva is expected to continue at left wing-back, although Jack Burroughs is an option.

Coventry were no match for Premier League side Ipswich on Saturday as three first-half goals set the Tractor Boys on course for a 4-1 away win. Attention now turns back to the Championship, where Fr...

arts.ttl: Wright, Sheaf, Mason-Clark, Bidwell - Coventry City injury news and expected return dates - CoventryLive
arts.sum: Both bosses have had their share of injury problems in recent months but for Lampard, at least, there appears to be a great deal of light at the end of the tunnel for three of his key players to return, having been without Haji Wright, Ben Sheaf and Ephron Mason-Clark for a number weeks and, in Wright’s case, months. Like the Sky Blues, QPR have picked up since a change of manager with new boss Marti Cifuentes lifting the West London club from second from bottom to 13th place in the table, level on points with 12th placed City who, similarly, have enjoyed an upwards surge under Frank Lampard.

arts.ttl: Coventry City vs QPR predictions: Goals at both ends expected in Coventry
arts.sum: Coventry entertain QPR in the Championship on Tuesday and Alex Wrigley expects both teams to find the back of the net.

arts.ttl: Coventry City promotion winner dies, favourite taken ill and fourth kit verdict ahead of QPR bounce-back
arts.sum: Tudor began his football career playing for his local team Ilkeston Town before turning professional with Coventry, going on to score eight goals in 17 matches in 1966–67 to helping the Sky Blues win the Second Division title and promotion to the top flight under Jimmy Hill. Best wishes for a speedy recover go to former Coventry City player of the year John Fleck who was taken to hospital last Thursday night after collapsing on the pitch just minutes before kick-off in a League Two match.

arts.ttl: QPR suffer big injury setback as manager 'sad' for luckless former Coventry City loan man
arts.sum: Since opting to join QPR in the summer of 2022 rather than sign for the Sky Blues on a permanent deal, injuries have restricted him to 16 league appearances in his first season, 33 in his second and just 11 in the league during the current campaign.
